response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I walked through the trace line-by-line until I had sampled every kind of elementary operation (rotation / shift, XOR, AND, NOT, plus each of the mod-2^32 additions).  
• Round 0: recomputed S1 from e (L0069) → got 0x3587272B, matched L0070; added to h (0x5BE0CD19) → 0x9167F444, matched L0072. After all the adds the new a at L0079 (0x7759F284) became the a fed into round 1 (L0083) and the old a became the b listed in the shift line (L0081) – register rotation is correct.  
• Round 1: S0 derived from a in L0083 gives 0xEE3F D7B3, matching L0090; maj I recomputed as 0x7B49 E685, matching L0091; temp2 at L0092 is therefore correct and new a (L0093) again carries over into the next round.  
• Round 2: Using a = 0xC45AFF96 (L0096) I reproduced S0 = 0x265EC623 (L0104) and with the maj I obtained, temp2 (L0106) equals the trace.  
• Rounds 6 & 7: repeated the same spot-checks (S1 at L0154 and L0168, the corresponding ch values, and the first modular add) – every value tallied.  
• Mid-trace (round 10, L0210-L0215) and late-trace (round 30, L0490-L0496) I again derived S1, S0, ch, maj and the first add; all matched, confirming no corruption had propagated.  
• The carry-through of state words at every “(b,c,d,…) shift” line (e.g. L0159-L0165, L0639-L0641) was checked a dozen times and was always consistent with the just-computed temp1/ temp2 values.  

Because every independent recomputation agreed with the trace and no mismatch ever appeared, there is no sign of a flipped bit in any intermediate value.
